rulings.Case Title: REGINA M. THOMSON VS. THOMAS HARRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion For Assignment Order, Turnover Order And Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 7. Defendant'S Motion For Assignment Order, Turnover Order And Restraining Order Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Defendant's motion for a restraining order is granted. (CCP § 708.520.) Plaintiff is hereby restrained from assigning or otherwise disposing of the right to payment as to the amount of $ 70,915.24 of the judgment issued in favor of Plaintiff in Thomson v. Canyon (Alameda County Superior Court case no. RG08400707.) To the extent that Plaintiff's counsel may be entitled to any of amount subject to the restrained order pursuant to his attorney lien, Plaintiff may file and serve a motion to modify or vacate. (See CCP § 708.520(c).) All other requests for relief are denied. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK STONICH et al VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.,'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to November 4, 2013 on Court's own motion.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALCIDES VALLADARES PERLA VS. DMG ASET MANAGEMENT LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 1. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff fails to allege a duty, its breach, causation and damages. Plaintiff's request for leave to amend is granted as follows: Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to amend the complaint all allege in good faith causes of action for promissory estoppel. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CHUNG VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Bank Of America, N.A. And Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 2. Defendants' Bank Of America, N.A. And Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege all elements of each of the 13 causes of action; to allege facts showing tolling of statute of limitations; and to allege tender or exception from tender rule. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with CCP 1005 and CRC 2.108 in future. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. RAY K. SHAHANI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT RAY SHAHANI's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND to explain in good faith discrepancies between allegations in Complaint 2, 6 and FAC/SAC 2, 6. Specifically to explain why Wells Fargo asserted ownership of the premises based on March 4, 2013 foreclosure sale in Complaint and Bank of America asserted ownership of the same property based on the same March 4, 2013 foreclosure sale. Opposing party fails to cite any authority or provide any argument for the proposition that power of attorney grants ownership rights, i.e. 13 of SAC fails to explain the discrepancy described above. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RKI INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. EDWARD JELINEK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 3. PLAINTIFF RKI INVESTMENTS, LLC's Motion To Tax Costs GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to $72 incurred to "interview David Bellings about service of a subpoena to him." Motion is otherwise denied, subject to Defendants producing proof of service re: trial subpoenas and bills from process server re: trial subpoenas. Declaration with these documents attached should be filed with the Court no later than 9:00 a.m. on October 24, 2013. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SF 861 POST STREET,LLC VS. M.C. VAN ABKOUDE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT M.C. VAN ABKOUDE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery; Declaration GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted only as to RFAs 22, 23, 27 and 28. Plaintiff shall respond without limitation to its personal knowledge. Otherwise motion is denied. No sanctions.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SF 861 POST STREET,LLC VS. M.C. VAN ABKOUDE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT M.C. VAN ABKOUDE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Verification is sufficient under CCP 446. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that (1) verification must repeat the language of CCP 446 verbatim; and (2) Plaintiff must have personal knowledge regarding grounds for eviction alleged in "3 Day Notice."= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SF 861 POST STREET,LLC VS. M.C. VAN ABKOUDE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, And For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT M.C. VAN ABKOUDE's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, And For Sanctions; GRANTED. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses without objections within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $360. = (501/REQ)
